---
title: ""
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    css: language-toggle.css
---

<div class="language-toggle">
  <a href="about.html" class="language-btn language-btn-active">EN</a>
  <a href="about_fr.html" class="language-btn">FR</a>
</div>


### Research

My research investigates how digital capitalism reshape markets, cities, and inequalities. I use mixed methods, including ethnographic fieldwork, expert interviews, spatial analysis, and computational social science.

The main focus of my research is to analyze how digital technologies and financial capital transform housing markets, renewing social stratification and urban segregation. This work positions South Africa as a key site to understand how 21st-century markets intersects with long-standing, racialized patterns of wealth inequality and urban development.

The geography of finance is my second line of research. I explore how digitization is transforming financial markets, services, and centres, with an emphasis on FinTech ecosystems. I am a co-author of the *Atlas of Finance*, published at Yale University Press in 2024.

### Background

Before joining Cambridge, I was an [Urban Studies Foundation Postdoctoral Research Fellowship](https://www.urbanstudiesfoundation.org/) at the University of Oxford, where I previously worked as a Research Associate in Finance and Geography on the ERC project [Cities in Global Financial Networks](https://cordis.europa.eu/project/id/681337) (2021-2022). I served as a Lecturer in the Department of Geography at the École Normale Supérieure in Paris (2017-2021). I held visiting positions at the African Centre for Cities at the University of Cape Town (2016, 2023) and at the Department of Sociology at UC Berkeley (2024-2025). I am an early-career representative at the [Global Network on Financial Geography](https://www.fingeo.net/).

The majority of my education took place in France. I am a graduate of the École Normale Supérieure de Lyon, where I was a student fellow (*éleve normalien*), obtained my Master's degree in Geography & Planning, and passed the [agrégation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agr%C3%A9gation). I then completed my PhD at the University of Grenoble Alpes and at the research centre [Géographie-cités](https://geographie-cites.cnrs.fr/en/geographie-cites/) in Paris, where I remain a Research Associate. My dissertation received two national awards: the [Doctoral Prize in Urban Studies](https://www.urbanisme-puca.gouv.fr/prix-de-these-sur-la-ville-2021-a2141.html?lang=en) and the [Best Dissertation Award in Geography](https://www.cnfg.fr/vie-du-comite/prix-de-these-du-cnfg/).

I am very passionate about rugby, and keep an eye on the evolution of this sport into a [global industry](https://jmigozzi.github.io/rwcsquads2023/).
